justification: By-design and overseer-confirmed (T-6 corrective): AC-4 task-reference = the conventional-commit SCOPE position (feat(T-N):), which build MANDATES for every task. Verified in code (distinctTaskTokens, sdlc-check.mjs:936-958): scope-only matching is deliberate — whole-subject matching false-positived on docs-commit prose (the documented dogfood). The failure direction is SAFE: an unscoped implementing commit (feat: T-1) yields no match -> the task reads as no-commit -> BLOCK, never a silent pass; a second fix(T-N): commit is a non-atomic-history violation the exactly-one rule is meant to catch. Not a false pass.

Not in the threat model. Verified in code (run(), sdlc-check.mjs:63-80): the checker is a local, read-only dev/CI tool invoked by the pipeline stage with a spec path IT controls; there is no privilege boundary crossed — the caller already has the process file access. No untrusted/remote input reaches specPath. Path-traversal by someone who already controls the argv and runs the process locally is not an escalation. Advisory (single model).

By-design (T-7, reviewed): the artifact is the AC->proof map; parseSpec deliberately does not define NC ids (ID_DEFINITION_RE = AC|C|T). Per the spec verification map, NC-1/NC-2 are test-backed folded into the AC-11/AC-12 runs and NC-3/NC-4 are reviewer-checked at ship — so NCs ARE verified, just not via their own proof rows. Scoping AC-13 completeness to defined ACs is correct against the spec. Advisory (single model); logged as a possible follow-up if own-row NC proofs are ever wanted.

Advisory: the checker-invocation lines are illustrative skill PROSE templates with a <feature> placeholder, not executed code; the feature slug is an author-controlled spec-directory name, not external/attacker input. Recommend a follow-up to state a slug constraint ([A-Za-z0-9._-]+) and pass the path as a separate argv element, but no injection path exists in the shipped artifacts. Single model.

Tied to the ::952:: by-design decision: the AC-4 tests correctly assert that unscoped/prose task mentions do NOT count, because scope-position matching is the intended AC-4 semantics (overseer-confirmed). A test asserting feat: T-1 (no scope) counts would contradict the design. Not a coverage gap. Advisory.

Tied to the ::1073:: by-design decision: NC out-of-scope for proof-map completeness is intentional (NCs verified via AC-11/12 folding + ship review per the spec verification map). The test correctly pins that scope. Advisory.
